findings.title: Headline coverage test is lenient when CP00-135 is absent from the plan
findings.detail: The test 'covers the planned units' calls validateAuditComplianceCp135Coverage(plannedPack); if plannedPack is undefined (CP00-135 missing from closeout-pack-plan.json) the validator skips the plan-comparison block and returns {ok:true}, so the deepEqual still passes. Plan presence is only hard-enforced by validate-rp03-audit-architecture.mjs (`if (!cp135Plan) errors.push(...)`).
findings.recommendation: Assert plannedPack is defined (and included_units length === 150) inside the test so the catalog/plan binding is enforced at the unit-test layer too. Non-blocking because the validator gate already enforces presence.

findings.title: Hermes evidence packet hardcodes status 'passed' without execution
findings.detail: createAuditComplianceCp135HermesEvidencePacket maps every supplied command to {status:'passed'} without running it. Appropriate for a no-write readiness scaffold and does not write Hermes runtime, but the descriptor should not be mistaken for executed evidence.
findings.recommendation: Keep as descriptive scaffold; ensure downstream adjudication treats this as a planned-evidence template, not collected evidence.

findings.title: Contract schema_version bumped v0.1 -> v0.2
findings.detail: The only consumer updated in this diff (validate-rp03-audit-architecture.mjs) now requires v0.2. No other consumers are visible in the diff.
findings.recommendation: Confirm no other script/test/loader still pins 'law-firm-os.audit-compliance-contract.v0.1'.

findings.title: Validator omits two attestation fields from its no-write check
findings.detail: entry_readiness_no_write_attestation in the contract includes persists_idempotency_keys and executes_admin_access_review, but the validator's Object.entries check loop does not assert those two fields (catalog CP135_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION does cover them).
findings.recommendation: Add the two missing fields to the validator loop for full attestation coverage.

parsed_review.recommendation: Close CP00-135 after adjudicating non-blocking P3 findings; no P0/P1/P2 findings block production_ready.
parsed_review.rationale: CP00-135 is a clean no-write, synthetic-only Risk C audit/compliance entry readiness pack. All required boundaries hold: no real data, no audit event append/mutate/delete/query/export/render, no product state writes, no permission/query/export execution, no Hermes runtime write, no Claude execution (read-only descriptor with executes_review:false), HRX not split, LDIP not implemented. Code is pure frozen data structures and pure functions with only file reads in the test/validator. Unit math verified internally consistent: 150 units, range RP03.P00.M00.S01-RP03.P01.M08.S05, deliverable counts (impl 104, contract 3, security_audit 6, ui 19, fixture 3, test 9, hermes 3, claude 3) all sum and match assertions; first/last unit IDs and uniqueness check out. Sensitive field names are a redaction denylist, not leaked values. Findings are P3 (test leniency, descriptive-passed evidence, schema bump consumer check, minor validator gap); none block production_ready.
